---
title: "The Data"
subtitle: "18 macro variables with pseudo-real-time discipline"
---

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
variables = FileAttachment("_data/variable_metadata.json").json()
panel = FileAttachment("_data/panel_timeseries.json").json()
```

## Data sources

The panel combines Norwegian national statistics, central bank data, and global macro indicators. Every variable has a **publication lag** — the delay between the end of the reference month and when the data actually becomes available.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-variables
//| fig-cap: "Variable coverage and publication lags. Color indicates data source."

{
  const sourceColors = {
    "SSB": "#3498db",
    "FRED": "#e74c3c",
    "Norges Bank": "#2c3e50"
  };

  const data = variables.map(v => ({
    name: v.display_name,
    source: v.source,
    lag: v.publication_lag_days,
    is_target: v.is_target,
    first: v.first_available ? new Date(v.first_available) : null,
    description: v.description
  })).filter(d => d.first);

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 500,
    marginLeft: 170,
    marginRight: 60,
    x: {
      type: "utc",
      label: "Date range",
      domain: [new Date("1947-01-01"), new Date("2026-06-01")]
    },
    y: {
      label: null,
      domain: data.map(d => d.name).reverse()
    },
    marks: [
      // Bars from first_available to now
      Plot.barX(data, {
        x1: "first",
        x2: new Date("2026-03-31"),
        y: "name",
        fill: d => sourceColors[d.source] || "#999",
        fillOpacity: 0.7,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.name}\n${d.source}\nLag: ${d.lag} days\n${d.description}`
      }),
      // Target indicators
      Plot.text(data.filter(d => d.is_target), {
        x: new Date("2026-05-01"),
        y: "name",
        text: d => "target",
        fontSize: 9,
        fill: "#e74c3c",
        fontWeight: "bold"
      }),
      // Validation era
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2006-01-01")], {stroke: "#2ecc71", strokeWidth: 1.5, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
      Plot.ruleX([new Date("2016-01-01")], {stroke: "#e74c3c", strokeWidth: 1.5, strokeDasharray: "4,4"}),
      // Labels
      Plot.text([{x: new Date("2006-01-01"), text: "Validation start"}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", dy: -10, fontSize: 9, fill: "#2ecc71"
      }),
      Plot.text([{x: new Date("2016-01-01"), text: "Test start"}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", dy: -10, fontSize: 9, fill: "#e74c3c"
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

**Legend:** [SSB]{style="color:#3498db;font-weight:bold"} (Statistics Norway) | [FRED]{style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:bold"} (US Federal Reserve) | [Norges Bank]{style="color:#2c3e50;font-weight:bold"} (Norwegian Central Bank)


## Pseudo-real-time discipline

At each forecast origin, we only use data that would have been **actually available** on that date. This means applying publication lags: CPI is available 10 days after month-end, unemployment after 30 days, Euro area GDP after 90 days.

Use the slider below to explore which data is visible at any given forecast origin.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof originDate = Inputs.date({
  label: "Forecast origin",
  min: "2006-01-01",
  max: "2025-12-31",
  value: "2015-06-15"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
//| label: fig-realtime
//| fig-cap: "Data available at the selected forecast origin, respecting publication lags."

{
  const origin = new Date(originDate);

  const lagData = variables.map(v => {
    const lagMs = v.publication_lag_days * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
    const cutoff = new Date(origin.getTime() - lagMs);
    return {
      name: v.display_name,
      lag_days: v.publication_lag_days,
      cutoff: cutoff,
      source: v.source,
      months_available: Math.max(0, Math.round((cutoff.getTime() - new Date("1990-01-01").getTime()) / (30.44 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)))
    };
  });

  return Plot.plot({
    height: 480,
    marginLeft: 170,
    marginRight: 80,
    x: {
      type: "utc",
      label: "Last available observation",
      domain: [new Date("2004-01-01"), new Date(origin.getTime() + 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000)]
    },
    y: {
      label: null,
      domain: lagData.map(d => d.name).reverse()
    },
    marks: [
      // Origin line
      Plot.ruleX([origin], {stroke: "#e74c3c", strokeWidth: 2}),
      Plot.text([{x: origin, text: "Forecast origin"}], {
        x: "x", text: "text", dy: -10, fontSize: 10, fill: "#e74c3c", fontWeight: "bold"
      }),
      // Cutoff dots
      Plot.dot(lagData, {
        x: "cutoff",
        y: "name",
        fill: d => d.lag_days <= 1 ? "#2ecc71" : d.lag_days <= 30 ? "#f39c12" : "#e74c3c",
        r: 6,
        tip: true,
        title: d => `${d.name}\nLag: ${d.lag_days} days\nLast obs: ${d.cutoff.toISOString().slice(0, 10)}`
      }),
      // Lag labels
      Plot.text(lagData, {
        x: "cutoff",
        y: "name",
        text: d => `${d.lag_days}d`,
        dx: 15,
        fontSize: 9,
        fill: "#666"
      })
    ]
  });
}
```

**Color key:** [Fast (0-1 day)]{style="color:#2ecc71;font-weight:bold"} | [Medium (2-30 days)]{style="color:#f39c12;font-weight:bold"} | [Slow (31+ days)]{style="color:#e74c3c;font-weight:bold"}


## Variable details

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(variables.map(v => ({
  Variable: v.display_name,
  Source: v.source,
  "Lag (days)": v.publication_lag_days,
  Target: v.is_target ? "Yes" : "",
  Description: v.description
})), {
  sort: "Source",
  rows: 20,
  width: {Variable: 150, Source: 100, "Lag (days)": 80, Target: 50, Description: 300}
})
```
